summary: St. Stephen finale and soaring Help>Slip>Kenn make this Halloween show shine
blurb: The second set delivers a monster Saint Stephen, a scorching Help On The Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower, and an inspired percussion jam with Airto Moreira. Jerry’s guitar soars while Brent’s chime‑like keyboards sparkle throughout. The rhythm section locks in tightly, leading to a vibrant encore.
review: October 31, 1983 finds the Dead in top Halloween form at Marin County Veterans Auditorium. From the opening Wang Dang Doodle through a tight first set, the band builds momentum that explodes on the second set: the Help On The Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower sequence is described as “killer” and “soaring,” setting up an epic percussion jam with guest percussionist Airto Moreira that segues into a fiery Saint Stephen – the last live rendition of the song. Jerry Garcia’s guitar shines on St. Stephen and the Help‑Slip‑Kenn stretch, even if his vocal line slips on Help in places; Brent Mydland provides chime‑like keyboard sparkle and drives Not Fade Away, which several reviewers call the show’s MVP. Phil Lesh is “on top of his game,” laying down tight bass through the space jam, while Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann deliver wild, “weird drums” that keep the energy high. Despite a few technical quirks (a speed/timing splice on Wang Dang Doodle and occasional mix dominance), the performance’s highlights outweigh its flaws, making this Halloween concert a must‑listen for fans seeking classic Dead improvisation and tight set flow.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings praise the Help>Slip>Kenn sequence, Saint Stephen and the Airto percussion jam, indicating strong positive sentiment. A few mixed comments note minor vocal flubs, speed errors, and one low‑energy recording, tempering enthusiasm but not overturning it. Net effect is a modest positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Above‑average soundboard capture with clear instrument separation; includes all standout moments despite a minor splice, making it the most balanced and complete version.

key_highlights: Killer Help On The Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower sequence
Fiery Saint Stephen as the climactic second‑set finale
Airto Moreira guest percussion jam that fuels the space segment
Brent Mydland’s chime‑like keyboards adding sparkle throughout

band_performance.Jerry: Guitar soars on Saint Stephen and the Help‑Slip‑Kenn stretch; occasional vocal flub on Help, and his tone can be buried in the mix on St. Stephen.
band_performance.Phil: Tight, top‑of‑his‑game bass lines drive the space jam and hold the groove solidly throughout.
band_performance.Brent: Chime‑like keyboards sparkle, especially during second‑set jams; heavy mix on St. Stephen but shines on Not Fade Away and Wang Dang Doodle.
band_performance.Mickey: Wild “weird drums” with Airto’s percussion create a legendary space segment; notable tapping on Estimated Prophet.
band_performance.Billy: Provides powerful drumming alongside Mickey, contributing to the high‑energy jam that propels St. Stephen.
